The Arsenal Football Club is an English professional football club based in Islington, North London, England. They compete in the Premier League, the top tier of English football. In domestic football, Arsenal have won 13 league titles (including one unbeaten title), a record 14 FA Cups, 2 League Cups, 17 FA Community Shields and a Football League Centenary Trophy. In European football, they have won one European Cup Winners' Cup and one Inter-Cities Fairs Cup. In terms of trophies won, it is the third-most successful club in English football.

Now you know why I spend 30-60 minutes stretching every day. Getting surgery on both hips before turning 35 feels insane but it really is the only option I have left if I want to get back to playing sports and running. I will be doing short and long form content covering my recovery, why I decided to do this, what my condition was, and hopefully eventually what playing sports is like afterward. If you know struggling with hip arthritis please share this with them!

U-turns are one of the most effective leash training exercises you can practice with your dog  especially strong, confident breeds like Rottweilers 

When you incorporate U-turns into your walks, you’re teaching your dog to tune in to your movement and direction. Each turn helps your dog learn that following your lead keeps the walk calm, structured, and rewarding.

This exercise strengthens:
➡️ Focus – your dog learns to watch your body language.
➡️ Impulse control – breaking fixations on distractions.
➡️ Engagement – your dog pays more attention to you, not the environment.

Consistent U-turn practice transforms pulling into partnership  creating a calm, connected walk that’s enjoyable for both ends of the leash. 

#rottweilertraining #leashtraining #dogtrainingtip #structuredwalk #calmdog #rottie #obedientdog #dogtraining #balancedtraining #canine #rottweiler #heel

While racism fueled protests after a Black family (The Spencer’s) moved into Rosedale in Queens NY, a group of Black children saw something different.

They saw the waving of American flags and crowds marching through the streets. 

They thought it was a parade.

As the white youth of Rosedale shouted “white power” & “white resistance” they saw the black bikers and began to persecute them. 

You can see the pain and justified rage in the little black girl’s eyes as she plotted revenge in her heart. 
But I love how the older girl understood that hate— but didn’t choose to succumb to it herself and did her best to sympathize for the distorted racist white youths.

Revenge will always make sense, but it will NEVER make even. 

At the end of the clip you see Mr Spencer speak on not teaching his own children hate. Despite his house being burned and pipe bombed recently for just moving into the community. 

Film: Bill Moyers’ Journal- Rosedale “The Way It Is” 🎥 

#BlackHistoy #BlackHistoryMonth #happyblackhistorymonth #NewYork #Fyp

📍 This is not something I do often,
just an occasional reset for utensils that get heavy use or hold onto odors (garlic, onion, oils). Bamboo is botanically a grass, but in food-safety and material science it’s classified and regulated as a wood-like, plant-based material; both bamboo and hardwood are porous and absorb oils, moisture, and smells over time. Short, occasional boiling is different from long soaking or dishwashing, which actually degrades fibers faster due to heat, detergents, and extreme drying. Dishwashers don’t sanitize with bleach by default and can shorten the lifespan of both bamboo and hardwood. Conditioning afterward (mineral oil, beeswax blends, or coconut oil) is acceptable when applied thinly and wiped off, if coconut oil isn’t your preference or you have allergies, mineral oil works great too. Buying new or using the dishwasher is a personal choice,
this is simply what works for my kitchen and helps extend the life of tools I already own. Like most kitchen care topics, there’s nuance, not one single “right” way. 
.
.
.
In case no one told you… wooden spoons need a deep clean too. 🪵✨

Because wood is porous, it can hold onto old oils + odors over time, so every once in a while I “reset” mine the simple way (no harsh chemicals), then seal them so they last longer.

How to deep clean + condition wooden spoons

What you need: a large pot, water, towel, coconut oil

1. Boil: Fill a pot with enough water to cover the spoons. Bring to a gentle boil and boil 5–10 minutes.
2. Remove + dry: Carefully take them out, pat dry, then let them air-dry completely for 24 hours (this part matters!).
3. Oil: Rub a thin layer of coconut oil over the spoon (handle too).
4. Let it soak: Leave it 1–2 hours or overnight.
5. Wipe off excess: Buff off any extra oil and you’re done.

That cloudy water you see sometimes? That’s buildup leaving the wood. 👀

Save this for your next kitchen reset + tell me… when’s the last time you cleaned yours? 🤍

buttons.text: Boiling may cause warping and cracking and could exacerbate the problem. Best to wash with soap and water, dry them and then oil them.

buttons.headline_text: The Right Way to Clean Wooden Spoons (So They'll Last As Long As Possible)

John Cena doesn’t carry a second watch to tell time.
He keeps it with him to tell perspective.

On one side: “Comparison is the thief of joy.”
A reminder for the moments you feel like you’re not enough.

On the other: “Memento mori.”
A reminder to stay humble when ego gets loud.

This is mindset mastery.
When confidence dips → remember your worth.
When ego rises → remember you’re human.

The best athletes don’t live at either extreme.
They live grounded. Focused. Grateful.

Perspective isn’t something you find.
It’s something you practice daily.

Save this for the days you need the reminder.

#perspective #johncena #mindset

The things your inspector won’t mention — but you’ll definitely HATE once you move in:

• Check water pressure in every faucet and shower, especially upstairs. Trying to rinse shampoo out of your hair under weak water pressure is straight stress.

• Flip every light switch and make a mental note of its location. I’ve seen bathrooms where the fan switch comes before the light, and basements where you have to walk halfway into the dark just to turn it on. It’s small, but it’ll irk you every single time.

• Open every window and make sure they open, lock, and seal properly. Replacing windows can easily cost $20,000 or more per home.

• Find the water shut-off valves for your showers or built-in tubs. There should be an access panel behind it, usually in the closet of the room next to the bathroom. If there isn’t, that’s a red flag. I’ve seen homes where the only way to repair a leak was by busting through a kitchen backsplash or tearing out shower tile.

• Look in each room to see how many air vents there are. A large bedroom with one vent means you’ll be freezing in the winter and melting in the summer.

• Check cell service in every room, especially where you’ll work or sleep.

• Test the garage — not just the opener, but whether two cars actually fit with room to open the doors.

• Stand in the kitchen during “family hour.” Can someone open the fridge while another loads the dishwasher? If not, that layout will make busy mornings and dinner time a headache.

• Listen for traffic noise and barking dogs with the windows open.

• Pay attention to morning and evening light — early sunlight might sound nice until it’s in your eyes every morning.

• Notice where the outdoor spigots and outlets are. You don’t want to drag 100 feet of hose or extension cord every time you need one.

• Check how far your trash cans have to go to the curb. sounds dumb until you’re dragging your bins down a hill in January.

 It’s not just about saving money it’s about protecting your peace. These details might seem minor, but they decide whether your home brings you peace or problems.

The air inside our homes can be 3-5 times more polluted than the air outdoors. During these winter months, take some extra care to improve your home’s air quality.
